summary: Operations not being properly rejected with useUnifiedTopology
description:
If useUnifiedTopology is set while connected to a replicaset (MongoDB Atlas) the driver sometimes fails to properly reject operations when internet connection is lost. The operations is being kept as pending and then successfully executing when connection is reestablished after several minutes later. The expected result would be for the operation to throw a MongoTimeoutError for server selection after serverSelectionTimeoutMS has expired. The error scenario seems to happen when the operation is executed after the disconnection but before the first serverHeartbeatFailed event.

Besides driver version 3.3.5, this is still reproducible reproducible on master (commit 994c076af3532d753932b5b271f3ad57c5ae4b2a).

Reproduction steps on Node.js REPL:

 
{code:java}
$ node
Welcome to Node.js v12.10.0.
Type ".help" for more information.
> MongoClient = require('mongodb').MongoClient; client = new MongoClient(`mongodb+srv://${dbUser}:${dbPasswd}@${dbHost}/${dbName}`, { useUnifiedTopology: true, serverSelectionTimeoutMS: 2000, connectTimeoutMS: 2000 }); oldEmitter = client.emit; client.emit = (...args) => { console.log(args[0]); oldEmitter.apply(client, args); }; client.connect().then(conn => db = conn.db()).catch(console.log);
Promise { <pending> }
topologyOpening

serverOpening
serverOpening
serverOpening
serverDescriptionChanged
topologyDescriptionChanged
open
authenticated
serverDescriptionChanged
topologyDescriptionChanged
serverDescriptionChanged
topologyDescriptionChanged // Internet connection cut down
> db.db().collection('users').find().project({_id:1}).limit(2).toArray().then(console.log).catch(console.log)
Promise { <pending> }
serverHeartbeatStarted

summary: Resync bson-corpus array.json to fix duplicate test names

summary: Spec tests should use the same format for bulk writes
description:
There are three changes, the first two are breaking. 

The two breaking changes are in three command-monitoring tests: "bulkWrite.yml", "unacknowledgedBulkWrite.yml" and "insertMany.yml":

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/bebf9b688f64afdec46a860f102bad0841b04c02

The format of the "requests" array and "ordered" option changes from this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - insertOne: 
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        ordered: false
{noformat}
To this:
{noformat}
    operation:
      name: "bulkWrite"
      arguments:
        requests:
          - name: "insertOne"
            arguments:
              document: { _id: 4, x: 44 }
        options:
          ordered: false
{noformat}
This brings Command Monitoring tests in line with the Retryable Writes and forthcoming Transactions tests.

The non-breaking change is in the Retryable Writes tests:

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c0ce96daac5661f2603bfa52c9b3f683c176a292

Before, the tests said in prose that all MongoClients should be created with retryWrites=true. Now, the tests include the following YAML:
{noformat}
        clientOptions:
            retryWrites: true
{noformat}
The clientOptions are the same for all Retryable Writes tests. Interpreting this field now will prepare you for the Transactions tests, which make real use of the field.

description: See DRIVERS-779 for details.

summary: Must perform server selection if using sessions with topology that don't support them
description: Presently we make no checks after {{startSession}} as to whether we are presently connected to a topology that supports them. This breaks with spec. We must ensure this is the case, and if not we must perform server selection at this time in order to make a single attempt to find a suitable node.

summary: change stream should inherit properties of parent
description: The {{ChangeStream}} class currently stores a reference to the parent that created it ({{Db}}, {{Collection}} or {{MongoClient}}) in order to construct operations in the {{CommandOperation}} used for its cursor. Instead, it should save off copies of the relevant inheritable properties (read/write concern, read preference, etc) and act as its own parent for the cursor's operation.

summary: Convert FindOperation to use CommandOperation
description: The find operation is the only read operation that currently does not use the {{CommandOperation}} subclass, due to needing to optionally support sending legacy {{OP_QUERY}}. This change is involved, but must be done.

summary: Add missing tests for aggregation pipeline in bulk write updates
description: See DRIVERS-744 for details.

summary: Authentication fails if authSource not specified in URI
strict parsing of extended JSON $numberInt reads value as string
Add SDAM test for incompatible server becoming compatible
description: See DRIVERS-923 for details.

summary: Clarify behavior when "me" field doesn't match any values in "hosts" array
description: See DRIVERS-795 for details.

summary: Add RetryableWriteError error labels to retryable transaction tests
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Mongos currently doesn't support filling in errorLabels on a failCommand fail point, this adds them explicitly to the retryable transaction tests.
    See DRIVERS-826 for updated details.

summary: bson-ext Cannot compile on ARM platform
breaking change or show stopper bug in createCollection (node.js driver 3.6.0) - MongoError thrown
duck typing ObjectId can lead to potential type errors
description:
A change to support multiple versions of the {{bson}} module concurrently led to the use of duck typing the {{ObjectId}} type. In this situations we cannot depend strictly on duck-typing, but must ensure values exists and are the correct type before attempting to use them, lest we experience {{TypeError}}s

- https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/master/lib/objectid.js#L88
- https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/master/lib/objectid.js#L341

summary: Update AWS keys in FLE tests
description: See DRIVERS-1365 for updated details.

summary: Breaking changes must increase major version
description:
3.6.0 contains breaking changes and we are having problems because of it. Some packages depending on node-mongodb stopped working eg: winston-mongodb.

Please check
[https://github.com/winstonjs/winston-mongodb/issues/175]

summary: Convert metamocha into a mocha plugin
description: Metamocha should be a plugin that is either loaded via {{mocha -r}} or loaded via some sort of mocha plugin API.

summary: Convert metamocha "filters" to mocha plugin
Convert mongodb-test-runner to a mocha plugin
Fix either API or unit tests for cursor.count
description:
count has a required `applySkipLimit` parameter
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/cursor.js#L982

However, in our unit tests, we sometimes omit the parameter:
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/test/functional/cursor_tests.js#L211

This can cause problems for executeOperation.

summary: Refactor spec test runners into their own common functions
description: A number of spec tests use common runners, but we've written one for each. The server selection and max-staleness runners have been mostly merged, but live inside a {{server_selection_spec_tests.js}} file together, and the APM + CRUD + Transactions tests all use a similar runner as well. These should all be moved into common locations, and optionally exported for use in {{native}}

summary: Implement rigorous auth integration tests
Tests in replset_operations_tests are broken
description: There are 3 tests currently skipped in replset_operations_tests because these tests are broken. We should fix these tests and then reenable them.

summary: Remove travis filter from metadata ui
description: The mocha runner metadata_ui.js has a filter for travis ci, since we no longer use travis we can drop the code for the filter and remove it from any tests that specify it.

summary: Remove null check in `isSDAMUnrecoverableError`
description: A check was added to see if an error was {{null}} in {{isSDAMUnrecoverableError}} to support "drain mode" for the legacy pool in conjunction with the unified topology. This is no longer necessary once CMAP is in place.

summary: Update version of dependency "bl" due to vulnerability
description:
A buffer over-read vulnerability exists in bl <4.0.3, <3.0.1 and <2.2.1 which could allow an attacker to supply user input (even typed) that if it ends up in consume() argument and can become negative, the BufferList state can be corrupted, tricking it into exposing uninitialized memory via regular .slice() calls.

 

[https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-pp7h-53gx-mx7r]

summary: Add URI option to disable certificate revocation checking
description: See DRIVERS-975 for details.
